evidence_summary.detail: Eight services use CI, Pact contracts, masked data, and partial Playwright coverage.

maturity_assessment.evidence: A service-level strategy exists and contract tests are used.
CI runs unit, integration, contract, and partial E2E tests.
PR gates block, but release criteria need tighter evidence.
Masked test data is available across lower environments.
Dashboard exists but lagging indicators are incomplete.
Developers own unit and contract tests.

test_strategy.rationale: Microservices require heavier integration and contract coverage than UI E2E.

test_data_strategy.synthetic_data: Generate service-specific payloads from contract examples.
test_data_strategy.masking_policy: Mask regulated data before lower-environment replication.
test_data_strategy.reset_policy: Reset service databases before contract and nightly gates.

quality_gates.on_failure: Service owner fixes or requests architecture review.
QA lead files a release-readiness risk.
Release manager blocks release until evidence is green.
On-call engineer pauses rollout or rolls back.

roadmap.success_criteria: Every service reports the same leading metrics.
Critical contracts cover provider and consumer expectations.

roadmap.focus: Strengthen release gate and performance baseline.
roadmap.success_criteria: Release gate blocks unmet SLA and security evidence.

roadmap.focus: Use dashboard trends for continuous improvement.

priority_actions.action: Add missing lagging indicators to the shared dashboard.
Automate masked data reset for contract and nightly gates.
Tighten release gate evidence for load and security criteria.

guardian.reason: All required sections are present with evidence and target gaps.
